introText: Projects I've contributed to across e-commerce, SaaS, cultural platforms, and business tools — as part of agency teams and as a freelancer.

projects.name: Fest Forum
projects.description: A digital collaboration platform for Poland's festival industry, built for Kraków Festival Office and the National Centre for Culture. The platform serves as a resource hub where festival organizers share best practices, access reports on copyright, sustainability, and crowdfunding, and participate in events remotely. I'm implementing fully WCAG-compliant views, a complex article builder with rich content editing, and working on integrating a custom backend. The project is ongoing.

projects.name: BUILD/LOG
projects.description: A construction project management SaaS that tracks the full lifecycle of building projects — from excavation to final handover. Built as part of a team, I integrated a custom PHP backend with a React and React Query frontend, building out complex data flows for project timelines, forms, filterable tables, and detailed views across multiple project phases.

projects.name: The Makery
projects.description: A multilingual admin dashboard and marketplace for an artisan workshop platform with hundreds of existing users. I was responsible for the transition from an aging, slow admin interface to a modern tech stack in Next.js — rebuilding data management views, user flows, and the core marketplace functionality while keeping the platform operational for its existing user base throughout the migration.

projects.name: BHT Startup Hub
projects.description: The website for Berlin University of Technology's startup incubator — a lean accelerator that has supported over 300 founders with coaching, coworking space, and equity-free scholarships. I built the custom frontend integrated with a WordPress backend, delivering pages for their programs (Berlin Startup Stipendium, EXIST grants), event calendars, news, and partner showcases.

projects.name: Navarra Lab
projects.description: The company portfolio website for Navarra Lab — the design studio and software house where I work. Showcases projects for clients like DMEXCO (Red Dot award winner), Zalando Connected Retail, The Makery, and others. This site is also the foundation that my personal portfolio is built on — a v2 evolution of the same codebase with custom animations, smooth scrolling, and a bilingual content system.

projects.name: Ultramaryna
projects.description: A cultural events discovery platform for the GZM metropolis in Silesia, operating since 2001 as a free guide to the region's cultural life. The platform lets users browse events across theater, music, visual arts, and film — with filtering by type, location, and date. I built the frontend for the digital platform, including the event browsing experience, newsletter integration, and the magazine content section.

projects.name: Murat Yumak
projects.description: A bilingual e-commerce platform for a Berlin-based curator of vintage streetwear classics from the 80s to the present. The site operates like a museum meets boutique — each item comes with cultural context and provenance, and objects remain viewable even after being sold. I built the bilingual (EN/DE) Shopify-integrated frontend with advanced filtering by brand, size, condition, and year, along with the archival browsing experience that sets the shop apart from typical resale platforms.

projects.name: Wykonczymy
projects.description: A management tool for a family-run construction and renovation company in Warsaw that also operates an online tile shop. I built a system covering project reporting, money flow tracking, and email automation — replacing scattered spreadsheets and manual processes with a centralized operational view tailored to how the business actually works.

projects.name: Moodbox
projects.description: A material sampling platform for interior designers and project professionals. Designers browse curated collections of tiles, textiles, wood flooring, and mosaics from partner brands like Cersanit, Paradyż, and Dunin — select samples, and receive them in organized boxes. I built the full platform with Shopify integration for the ordering flow, a custom Payload CMS backend for managing partner content and collections, plus email campaign tooling, surveys, and webhook integrations.

projects.name: Olej.to
projects.description: A lifestyle blog platform about the struggles and joys of simple life — blending humor, travel stories from Sri Lanka to Cambodia, and personal reflection. I implemented a WordPress backend for content management paired with a custom Svelte frontend, delivering a clean, minimal reading experience that prioritizes typography and quick scanning across categories like humor, serious essays, and personal stories.
